intent: Write a 'daily-driver toy recommendations' guide for parents of 5-to-8-year-olds. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) the One Stop Market shopping site — browse the toys-games category and collect at least 4 products across different play modes (board game, construction/STEM, creative/art, and outdoor/active). Record price, rating, and review count for each. (2) Reddit — browse /f/AskReddit AND /f/LifeProTips for posts about which toys get played with long-term, screen-time alternatives, or toys parents regret. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, and comment count. The report must: (a) present each storefront pick with a short play-value argument, (b) cross-check each pick against community patterns (toys that get abandoned fast, toys with hidden costs, toys that break), (c) organize the final picks by 'stays fun for months' versus 'novelty-only', (d) close with a $100 birthday-gift bundle. Cite every price, rating, and community claim.
author_notes: Shopping (toys-games) + Reddit (AskReddit + LifeProTips). Longevity vs novelty framing forces categorization reasoning.
